name: Vegetable Lasagna
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stems from spinach. Wash leaves thoroughly; cook spinach in a small amount of boiling water for 5 to 8 minutes or until tender. Drain; place spinach on paper towels, and squeeze until barely moist. Finely chop spinach.
2. Combine chopped spinach with 2 tablespoons margarine and salt and pepper to taste; set aside.
3. Cook carrots and broccoli in boiling water to cover for 8 to 10 minutes or until tender; drain well. Combine cooked vegetables with spinach mixture; set aside.
4. Combine vegetable broth and milk in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat until warm; remove from heat, set aside.
5. Saute chopped onion in 1/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons margarine in a large skillet until tender. Add flour, and cook for 3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add warm broth mixture and wine; simmer for 5 minutes or until the mixture is thickened, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and set aside.
6. Combine cheeses. Arrange 5 lasagna noodles in a lightly greased 13x9x2-inch baking dish (grease with Pam-type product). Layer 1/4 white sauce, 1/2 vegetables, 1/3 cheese, then 1/4 white sauce again. Repeat layers. Spread tomato sauce over top. Sprinkle with remaining 1/3 cheese.
7. Bake uncovered at 350 degrees F for 35 minutes. Let lasagna stand 10 minutes before serving.
